provisions.title: Short title and commencement
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Transfer of Prisoners Regulations, 2004 and shall come into operation on the date of publication.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations unless the context otherwise requires— “ Act ” means the Transfer of Prisoners Act, 2004; Act No.10 of 2004 " administering state " means the state to which the sentenced prisoner may be, or has been, transferred in order to serve his or her sentence ; " appropriate authority " in relation to Tanzania; means the Minister or other person or authority designated by him for the purpose of these Regulations, and in relation to a foreign country means the authority responsible for the administration of the law relating to the transfer of prisoners; " designated country " means any country designated as such pursuant to the provisions of section 4 of the Act ; “ judgement ” means a decision or order of a court or tribunal imposing a sentence ; “ Minister ” means the Minister for the time being responsible for matters relating to prisoners; “ national ” means— (a) in relation to the Government of a designated country , a national as defined in its Municipal law; (b) in relation to the Government of the United Republic, a national as defined in its Citizens Act ; “ sentence ” means any punishment or measure involving deprivation of liberty ordered by a court or tribunal for a determinate or indeterminate period of time in the exercise of its criminal jurisdiction; “ sentenced prisoner ” means a Tanzania citizen serving a sentence in a designated country or a citizen of a designated country serving a sentence in the United Republic of Tanzania; “ sentencing state ” means the state in which the sentence was imposed on the sentenced prisoner who may be, or has been, transferred; “ transfer ” means transfer of a prisoner from a designated country to Tanzania or from Tanzania to a designated country ; and “ warrant ” means any judicial document authorizing the transfer of a prisoner to or from the United Republic. Part II – General principles governing transfer of prisoners

provisions.title: Criteria for transfer of prisoners
provisions.content: The basis for the transfer of prisoners shall be— (a) to develop mutual co-operation between states in the field of criminal justice in order to facilitate the return of foreign prisoners to their home countries to serve their sentences; (b) to enhance mutual respect for national sovereignty and jurisdiction.

provisions.title: Principles governing transfer of prisoners
provisions.content: The transfer of prisoners shall be effected subject to the following governing principles— (a) transfer may be requested by either the prisoner or his representative, sentencing state or the administering state ; (b) the transfer shall be dependent on the agreement of both the sentencing and administering state and should also be based on the consent of the prisoner; (c) in case the prisoner is incapable of freely determining his will, his legal representative shall be competent to consent to the transfer ; (d) transfer will generally not take place where a sentence has less than six months to run and should be granted also in cases of indetermipate sentences; (e) transfers will not be automatic but will only take place where all three agree that it is warranted by the particular circumstances of the individual case; (f) only the sentencing state may decide on an application for a review of the judgement , (unless the countries concerned agree otherwise) grant pardon, amnesty or commutation; (g) the transfer of prisoners under these Regulations shall not curtail the rights of such prisoners to subsequent pardon, commutation of sentence and reduction of sentence to be granted by the sentencing state after the transfer ; (h) the prisoner transferred for the enforcement of a sentence passed in the sentencing state may not be tried again in the administering state for a corresponding offence; and (i) the transfer of prisoners shall be initiated when all available local remedies have been exhausted. Part III – Conditions for transfer of prisoners

provisions.title: Conditions for transfer of prisoners
provisions.content: A sentenced prisoner may be transferred under these Regulations only on the following conditions:— (a) if that prisoner:— (i) is a national of the administering state ; or (ii) has close ties with administering state of a kind that may be recognized by that state for the purpose of these Regulations; and (b) if the judgement in respect of the offence committed is final; and (c) if at the time of receipt of the request for transfer , the sentenced prisoner still has at least six months of the sentence to serve or the sentence is indeterminate; and (d) if the transfer is consented to by the sentenced prisoner or, where in view of his or her physical or mental condition one of the parties considers it necessary, by a person entitled to act on behalf of the prisoner; and (e) if the acts or omissions on account of which the sentence has been imposed constitute a criminal offence according to the law of the administering state or would constitute a criminal offence if committed on its territory; and (f) if the sentencing and administering state agrees to the transfer . Part IV – Transfer of prisoners from the United Republic of Tanzania

provisions.title: Application for transfer of prisoners from Tanzania
provisions.content: (1) An application under sub-section 14(2) of the Act (in this part referred to as a "prisoner's application") shall be in or to the effect of Form No. 1. (2) Prisoner's application shall be signed by the prisoner or his legal representative in case the prisoner is incapable of freely determining his will and shall be forwarded to the Minister through the Principal Commissioner of Prisons and the Officer in-charge of prison where the prisoner is imprisoned. (3) A prisoner's application shall include statements as to— (a) family or near family support in the designated country to which the prisoner seeks to be transferred, including the availability of accommodation upon the prisoners release from prison; (b) family or other social circumstances that may benefit the welfare of the prisoner either during imprisonment or following release from prison; (c) medical reasons (if any) in support of the request; (d) prospects of employment following release from prison; and (e) any other matters which the prisoner wishes to put forward in support of the application. (4) The officer In-charge of a prison who receives a prisoner's application shall:— prepare and attach to the application a statement of convictions and sentences in respect of which the prisoner is currently detained in custody. Setting out any probation or non-parole period specified and the current estimated date of release by remission; prepare and attach to the application a comprehensive report on the prisoner's conduct and behaviour while in prison; forward the application to the Principal Commissioner of Prisons for consideration by the Minister .

provisions.title: Consideration for prisoner's application
provisions.content: (1) For the purposes of the Minister 's consideration of a prisoner's application, the Principal Commissioner of Prisons shall supply to the Minister such medical, psychiatric or psychological reports or assessments in respect of the prisoner as are or may be made available to it. (2) There shall be included in any report or assessment in respect of a prisoner furnished or caused to be furnished for the information of the Minister under these Regulations any further information available to the person or authority furnishing the report or assessment, or causing the same to be furnished, which, may be of assistance to the Minister in considering the prisoner's application.

provisions.title: Request to appropriate authority to accept transfer of prisoner
provisions.content: (1) Where following consideration of a prisoner's application for transfer to a designated country ; the Minister is of the opinion, that the prisoner should be transferred, he shall make a written request to the appropriate authority of that country to accept the transfer of the prisoner. (2) A written request made under sub-regulation (1) for the transfer of prisoner shall be accompanied by the following particulars— (a) the name, sex, date and place of birth, or if date of birth is not known the approximate age of the prisoner; (b) the prisoner's address if any, in Tanzania; (c) a certified copy of the judgment or other order of the court; (d) a statement of the facts and circumstances upon which the conviction and sentence or other order were based; (e) the nature of the sentence , if any, its date of commencement and duration; (f) any medical or other report pertaining to the prisoner including a report of his treatment in Tanzania together with any recommendation for further treatment in the designated country ; (g) the address of the prisoner's last residence; (h) full names and addresses of three referees who are citizens of the designated country and are at the mateiiai time residing in the designated country ; and (i) any other information which the Minister may require to enable him to consider the desirabily of a transfer . (3) A written request issued under sub-regulation (1) shall be accompanied by a copy of: (a) the prisoner's application or certified copy of the application; (b) the reports, assessments and other information referred to in Regulation 5 and 6 in relation to the prisoner; and (c) a statement setting out the matters upon which the Minister 's opinion is based.

provisions.title: Warrant
provisions.content: (1) A warrant issued under section 14 of the Act shall be in or to the effect of Form No. 2. (2) The transfer of the sentenced prisoner from the custody of the authorities of Tanzania into custody of the authorities of designated country shall take place on the territory of the United Republic. Part V – Transfer of prisoners to the United Republic of Tanzania

provisions.title: Request for transfer
provisions.content: (1) Where the Minister receives from the appropriate authority of a designated country a request for the transfer of a prisoner to Tanzania, the Minister prior to considering the request, shall consult the Attorney-General the substance of the request and seek the Attorney-General's comments in writing. (2) Any comments the Attorney-General desires to make shall be made within 14 days of receipt by the Attorney-General of the advice of the substance of the request and shall be forwarded to the Minister . (3) In determining the request for transfer made under sub-regulation (1) , the Minister shall not agree to a transfer where the prisoner has less than six months of the sentence remaining to be served except on exceptional Circumstances.

provisions.title: Obligation to furnish information and language
provisions.content: (1) For the purposes of the Minister 's consideration of a request under section 5(1) of the Act , the appropriate authority of a designated country may (or, if the Minister so requests, shall) supply to the Minister — (a) the name, sex, date and place of birth, of the sentenced prisoner , or if the date of birth is not known the approximate age of the prisoners; (b) the prisoner's address if any in Tanzania; (c) a certified copy of the judgment or other order of the court; (d) a statement of the facts and circumstances upon which the conviction and sentence or other order were based; (e) the nature of the sentence , if any, its date of commencement and duration; (f) any medical or other report pertaining to the prisoner including a report of his treatment in the designated country together with any recommendation for further treatment in Tanzania; (g) the address of the prisoner's last residence; (h) full names and addresses of three referees who are citizens of the United Republic of Tanzania and are at the material time residing in Tanzania; and (i) any other information which the Minister may require to enable him to consider the desirability of a transfer . (2) If the sentenced prisoner has expressed his interest for being transferred to the United Republic, the designated country shall, on request, communicate to the United Republic the information referred to in subregulation (1) . (3) Information under sub-regulation 1 and 2 shall be furnished in the official language of the party to which it is addressed. (4) The sentenced prisoner shall be informed, in writing, of any action taken by the designated country or by the united Republic under the preceding sub-regulations, as well as of any decision taken by either country, on a request for his transfer .

provisions.title: Requests and replies
provisions.content: (1) Requests and applications for transfer and replies shall be made in writing. (2) Requests, applications, replies and any other communications under these Regulations shall be conducted through diplomatic channels and shall be addressed to competent authorities of the parties.

provisions.title: Supporting documents
provisions.content: (1) The United Republic of Tanzania, if requested by the designated country , shall send to it— (a) a document or statement indicating whether the sentenced prisoner satisfies the requirement of regulation 4(a) ; (b) a copy of the relevant law of the United Republic which provides that the acts or omissions on amount of which the sentence has been imposed in the designated country constitute a criminal offence according to the law of the United Republic or would constitute a criminal offence if committed in its territory. (2) If the transfer is requested, the designated country shall provide the following documents to the United Republic, unless either state has already indicated that it will not agree to the transfer (a) a certified copy of the judgement and the law on which it is based; (b) a statement indicating how much of the sentence has already been served, including information on remission of sentence and any other factor relevant to the enforcement of the sentence ; and (c) a declaration containing the consent to the transfer as referred to in Regulation 4(d) . (3) Either state may ask to be provided with any of the documents or statements referred to in sub-regulation 1 or 2 of this Regulation before making a request or taking a decision on whether or not to agree to the transfer . (4) Except as provided in sub-regulation 2(a) of this Regulation; all documents sent for the purpose and in accordance with these Regulations need not be certified.

provisions.title: Consent and its verification
provisions.content: (1) The designated country shall ensure that the person required to give consent to the transfer in accordance with Regulation 4(d) does so voluntarily and in writing, with full knowledge of the legal consequences thereof. The procedure for giving such consent shall be governed by the law of the designated country. (2) The designated country shall afford an opportunity to the United Republic of Tanzania to verify through a consul or other official agreed upon with the United Republic, that the consent is given in accordance with the conditions set out in sub-regulation 1 of this Regulation.

provisions.title: Enforcement and pardon
provisions.content: (1) The enforcement of the sentence shall be governed by the law of the United Republic of Tanzania. (2) Both the designated country and the United Republic of Tanzania shall be competent to grant pardon and amnesty.

provisions.title: Conversion of sentence
provisions.content: (1) In the case of conversion of sentence , the United Republic of Tanzania shall be entitled to adapt the sanction as to its nature or duration according to its national law, taking into due consideration the sentence passed in the designated country . (2) A sanction involving deprivation of liberty shall, however, not be converted to a pecuniary sanction.

provisions.title: Review of sentence
provisions.content: The United Republic of Tanzania shall be bound by the findings as to the facts in so far as they appear from the judgment imposed in the designated country . Thus the designated country has the sole competence for a review of the sentence . Part VI – Custody and transfer of prisoners

provisions.title: Continued enforcement
provisions.content: (1) In the case of continued enforcement, the United Republic of Tanzania shall be bound by the legal nature and duration of the sentence as determined by the designated country for the transferred sentenced prisoner . (2) If, however, the sentence imposed upon the transferred sentenced prisoner is by its nature or duration incompatible with the law of the United Republic of Tanzania or its law so requires, the designated country may, by court or administrative order, adapt the sanction to a punishment or measure presented by its own law for a similar offence. As to its nature, the punishment or measure shall, as far as possible, correspond with that imposed by sentence to be enforced. It shall not aggravate, by its nature or duration, the sanction imposed in the designated country , or exceed the maximum prescribed by the law of the United Republic of Tanzania.

provisions.title: Termination of enforcement
provisions.content: The United Republic of Tanzania shall terminate or cause to be terminated the enforcement of a sentence being served by the transferred sentenced prisoner as soon as it is informed by the designated country of any decision or measure as a result of which the sentence ceases to be enforceable.

provisions.title: Costs
provisions.content: The cost of transfer of the sentenced prisoners shall be defrayed in such proportions as may be agreed upon between Tanzania and designated country .

provisions.title: Transit prisoner
provisions.content: In any case involving a sentenced prisoner being transferred from a third country' to the territory of the designated country in order to serve the remainder of the sentence , the United Republic of Tanzania shall if so requested, facilitate the transit through its territory of sentence prisoner concerned. The state intending to make such a transfer shall give advance notice to the United Republic of Tanzania of such transit. Part VII – Miscellaneous provisions

provisions.title: Bilateral and multilateral agreement
provisions.content: Notwithstanding the provisions of these Regulations, any other mattes not covered in these Regulations shall be dealt with in accordance with any other bilateral or multilateral agreement entered into between the United Republic of Tanzania and foreign countries.

provisions.title: Prisoner’s nationally or residence
provisions.content: These Regulations leaves it to the discretion of the sentencing and administering states to decide whether the transfer should be effected to the country of the prisoner's nationality or residence.

provisions.title: Basis for transfer of prisoners
provisions.content: Notwithstanding the provision of these Regulations to the contrary, a transfer of a sentenced prisoner shall be made only on the basis of a final and definitive sentence having executive force and shall in no case lead to an aggravation of the situation of the prisoner. any country designated as such pursuant to the provisions of section 4 of the Act
a decision or order of a court or tribunal imposing a sentence
the Minister for the time being responsible for matters relating to prisoners
any punishment or measure involving deprivation of liberty ordered by a court or tribunal for a determinate or indeterminate period of time in the exercise of its criminal jurisdiction
a Tanzania citizen serving a sentence in a designated country or a citizen of a designated country serving a sentence in the United Republic of Tanzania
the state in which the sentence was imposed on the sentenced prisoner who may be, or has been, transferred
transfer of a prisoner from a designated country to Tanzania or from Tanzania to a designated country
any judicial document authorizing the transfer of a prisoner to or from the United Republic. Part II – General principles governing transfer of prisoners
